topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

Maya stared at the email on her screen, her jaw tightening. The subject line read, "Application for Senior Analyst Position." Her heart gave a strange, heavy thud against her ribs. She had poured weeks into tailoring her resume, highlighting every relevant achievement, and now, a name—Liam’s name—was front and center. She reread the cover letter he had submitted, noting the casual confidence in his tone. A slow, cold burn started in her chest. She picked up her coffee mug, the ceramic suddenly feeling hot and abrasive in her hand, and took a long, deliberate sip, trying to keep her expression neutral as she mentally cataloged every perceived slight.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" Mark muttered, the word barely escaping his lips as he leaned back in his chair, the springs groaning in protest. He looked at Sarah, who was scrolling through her phone with an unnervingly placid expression. He felt a sharp prickle behind his eyes, a tightening in his throat that made his voice sound gravelly. He had spent the last month refining his portfolio, perfecting his pitch for the role. Seeing her application, so polished and seemingly effortless, made him want to slam his laptop shut. He pushed his chair back, the scrape of metal on the floor sounding loud in the quiet office. He just wanted to walk away from this entire situation.

[story 3]

I watched the notification pop up. It was the confirmation email for the position I’d both been vying for. A sudden, unwelcome wave of heat washed over me, making my palms sweat. I clicked it open, and there it was: Liam’s name listed first. My breath hitched. I felt a strange, brittle tension building up in my shoulders, a stiffness that refused to relax. I quickly minimized the window, my fingers hovering over the mouse, unwilling to look again at the shared document. I stood up abruptly, needing to move, needing distance from the proximity of that shared opportunity.

He pushed his chair back, the scrape of metal on the floor sounding loud in the quiet office. He just wanted to walk away from this entire situation.

I watched the notification pop up. It was the confirmation email for the position I’d both been vying for. A sudden, unwelcome wave of heat washed over me, making my palms sweat. I clicked it open, and there it was: Liam’s name listed first. My breath hitched. I felt a strange, brittle tension building up in my shoulders, a stiffness that refused to relax. I stood up abruptly, needing to move, needing distance from the proximity of that shared opportunity.
